name: Calzone Di Broccoli
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Mild Italian Sausage, Chopped, browned and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Proof the yeast by blending it with 1/4 C warm water. Let stand until it bubbles.
2. Combine flour, salt and sugar. Add 1 tbsp olive oil, blending by rubbing the mixture between the palms of your hands.
3. Make a well in dry ingredients. Add yeast mixture and work in by hand. Add remaining water, gradually. Blend well and gather up.
4. Place 1 tsp olive oil on a wooden board. Knead dough on board until smooth and satiny. Place dough in a deep bowl and cover with a piece of plastic wrap and a towel. Let rise in a warm place for 1 1/2 hours or until doubled in bulk. Punch down and knead for a minute.
5. Divide into six equal parts. On a floured board, roll out each section into an 8"x10" rectangle.
6. About 30 minutes before dough has finished rising, prepare filling.
- Clean and wash the broccoli. Separate stalks. Soak in cool water with 1 tsp salt for 15 minutes. Rinse in cool water. Cook in a large pot of boiling water for 5 minutes. Drain well and chop. Place in a large bowl.
7. Add cheese, garlic and sausage, salt and pepper to the broccoli mixture and mix well. Divide into six equal portions.
8. On the long side of the dough, place about 2/3 C (tightly packed) filling in a strip 1 inch away from three edges of the dough. Flatten and smooth out the filling with a spoon.
9. Brush half of the dough edges with egg mixed with water. Fold the empty side of the dough over filling to evenly meet the edges on the other side. Tamp down edges to seal well.
10. With floured fingers turn edges under 1/4 inch. Repeat on others. Sprinkle two baking tins with cornmeal. Place three calzones on each tin (not touching each other). Tamp down edges once more to ensure a seal.
11. Brush tops of calzones with remainder of beaten egg mixture. Prick the tops with a round toothpick, making holes 1/2 inch apart all over the top. Do not pierce through to bottom dough.
12. Bake in a preheated 375 degree oven for 30 minutes or until golden brown. Serve hot or cold.
Alternate filling:
- 1 1/2 lb drained ricotta cheese
- 3 slices proscuitto, diced
- 3 tbsp Romano cheese
- 3 tbsp freshly grated Parmesan cheese
- 2 eggs
- Salt and black pepper to taste
- 2 tbsp minced fresh parsley
- 1 clove garlic, minced
Mix ingredients and use in place of filling above.
